fieldGroups.description: Medication safety, allergies, injectables, and O0110 special treatments.

fieldGroups.label: Functional And Therapy Status
fieldGroups.description: GG/M functional scoring, therapy need, discipline frequency, and goals.

fieldGroups.label: Symptom And Body System Review
fieldGroups.description: Body-system review sections that QA checks for consistency and completeness.

fieldGroups.label: Risk And Safety Assessments
fieldGroups.description: Fall, wound, hospitalization, and safety-related risk tools.

fieldGroups.label: Care Plan And Coordination
fieldGroups.description: Plan-of-care orders, patient summary, skilled interventions, and care plan content.

fieldGroups.label: Supplementals And History
fieldGroups.description: Past medical history, caregiver support, living situation, immunizations, and supplementals.

fieldGroups.label: Low Priority Admin Reference
fieldGroups.description: Identity, agency, physician, and demographic reference values that should not dominate QA.

sourceHighlights.title: Homebound reason
sourceHighlights.summary: Patient is homebound due to status post left total knee replacement and assistance needed for safe mobility and transfer. High risk for fall and injury.

sourceHighlights.title: Medical necessity
sourceHighlights.summary: Following a left total knee replacement surgery scheduled for April 2, 2026. Primary diagnosis is osteoarthritis of the left knee, necessitating surgical intervention. Patient is homebound due to post-surgery, experiencing significant functional limitations, including reduced mobility, pain management needs, and the necessity for assistance with activities of daily living (ADLs).
supportingSections: Primary Reason for Home Health Medical Necessity

sourceHighlights.title: Diagnoses and possible coding support references

draftNarratives.label: Homebound Reason
draftNarratives.draft: Patient is homebound due to status post left total knee replacement and assistance needed for safe mobility and transfer. High risk for fall and injury.

draftNarratives.label: Medical Necessity
draftNarratives.draft: Following a left total knee replacement surgery scheduled for April 2, 2026. Primary diagnosis is osteoarthritis of the left knee, necessitating surgical intervention. Patient is homebound due to post-surgery, experiencing significant functional limitations, including reduced mobility, pain management needs, and the necessity for assistance with activities of daily living (ADLs).

draftNarratives.label: Patient Summary / Clinical Narrative
draftNarratives.draft: Patient is scheduled for a left total knee replacement surgery on April 2, 2026, due to osteoarthritis. Post-surgery, the patient is expected to be homebound, requiring assistance with mobility and daily activities. The patient reports 7/10 pain at the left knee, which is constant and throbbing, affecting quality of life and day-to-day activities.

draftNarratives.label: Skilled Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Skilled nursing for PT/INR blood draws 2 times per week for 4 weeks. Physical therapy 3-5 times per week for 2-4 weeks.

draftNarratives.label: Plan For Next Visit
draftNarratives.draft: Continue physical therapy 3-5 times per week for 2-4 weeks.

draftNarratives.label: Care Plan Problems / Goals / Interventions
draftNarratives.draft: Problem: Post-surgical pain and mobility limitations. Goal: Improve mobility and reduce pain. Interventions: Physical therapy 3-5 times per week for 2-4 weeks, skilled nursing for PT/INR blood draws 2 times per week for 4 weeks.

draftNarratives.label: Patient / Caregiver Goals
draftNarratives.draft: Patient and caregiver goals include improving mobility, managing pain, and ensuring safe transfers and mobility with assistance.
